concepts.title: Tropospheric Temperature
concepts.description: The tropospheric temperature decreases with an increase in altitude. This is because the atmospheric pressure decreases with an increase in altitude, resulting in a decrease in temperature.

concepts.title: Classification of Climate
concepts.description: Climate can be classified into five main types: Tropical, Desert, Temperate, Continental, and Polar. Each type has distinct characteristics based on temperature and precipitation patterns.

concepts.title: Factors Affecting Climate
concepts.description: Climate is influenced by several factors, including latitude, altitude, distance from the sea, and prevailing winds. Latitude and altitude are the primary factors affecting climate.

concepts.title: Latitudinal Climate Zones
concepts.description: Use the mnemonic 'Tropical Temperate Tundra' to remember the three main latitudinal climate zones: Tropical (near the equator), Temperate (mid-latitudes), and Tundra (polar regions).

concepts.title: Confusing Climate and Weather
concepts.description: Remember that climate refers to long-term average atmospheric conditions, while weather refers to short-term conditions. Climate is not the same as weather, despite the two terms often being used interchangeably.

concepts.title: Köppen Climate Classification
concepts.description: The Köppen climate classification system categorizes climates into five main groups: A (tropical), B (dry), C (temperate), D (continental), and E (polar). Each group has distinct subcategories based on precipitation and temperature patterns.

concepts.title: Greenhouse Effect
concepts.description: The greenhouse effect is the process by which certain gases in the Earth's atmosphere trap heat from the sun, maintaining the planet's temperature at a level that supports life.

concepts.title: Climate Zones of India
concepts.description: India's climate zones include the tropical rainforest zone, the tropical monsoon zone, the semi-arid zone, the temperate zone, and the alpine zone. Each zone has unique characteristics and vegetation patterns.
